verbatim_text: Yeah, great question, I think. I think if you look at this, there are two approaches right now. Let's say you use some of the fancy rewards. You can also try to just use terminal rewards. Use the right priors. Where do the priors come from? Distillation. You can try to exhibit these right behaviors. I think that can work, but the sample efficiency there is pretty terrible. You need a lot of distillation data in order for the model to learn. In many cases, when you feed it this distillation, it just ends up and you train the model. It produces very long traces. In fact, that's some of the challenges that we run into when we train QDnano. This 234B thinking model is a distilled model.
